Name: aug_policy_ablation
Title: Ablating Data Augmentation Policy for Periapical Caries Classification
Experiment: Compare three lightweight augmentation settings in the current training pipeline: minimal augmentation, baseline augmentation, and stronger augmentation. Keep the model and optimizer fixed, then report AUC/F1/sensitivity/specificity to identify the most stable policy.

Name: imbalance_loss_comparison
Title: Class-Imbalance Loss Comparison for Periapical Caries Detection
Experiment: Benchmark standard cross-entropy, weighted cross-entropy, and focal loss under the same architecture and training budget. Focus on minority-class recall and F1 while tracking whether AUC remains stable.

Name: threshold_selection
Title: Decision-Threshold Optimization for Clinically Oriented Operating Points
Experiment: Keep the trained model fixed and tune prediction threshold on the validation set to target higher sensitivity vs higher specificity operating points. Report threshold-metric curves and compare against the default 0.5 threshold.

Name: mc_dropout_selective_prediction
Title: Uncertainty-Guided Selective Prediction via Monte Carlo Dropout for Safe Caries Detection
Experiment: Integrate a lightweight uncertainty quantification step directly into the existing training pipeline so the base command `python experiment.py --out_dir=run_i` automatically produces selective metrics. Inject a single dropout layer (p=0.3) before the classifier head in `build_model` to enable epistemic uncertainty estimation. After training, load `best_model.pth` and run K=20 stochastic forward passes on the test set: manually freeze all BatchNorm layers in `eval()` mode while keeping the injected dropout in `train()` mode. Aggregate logits to compute the predictive mean (for classification) and predictive entropy (for uncertainty). Implement a selective prediction routine that ranks test samples by entropy and abstains on the top X% most uncertain cases. Report risk-coverage curves, AUC, sensitivity, and ECE at 80%, 90%, and 95% coverage levels. Compare these against the deterministic baseline to quantify the clinical safety gain from deferring ambiguous cases to human experts, requiring zero changes to the execution command.

Name: tta_logit_averaging
Title: Test-Time Augmentation with Logit Averaging for Improved Calibration and Robustness
Experiment: Extend `build_transforms` to accept a `num_views` parameter that returns a list of K stochastic transforms (random crop, rotation, flip) for evaluation. Modify `evaluate` to loop over each view, extract raw logits, average them across views, then apply softmax and compute metrics. Add `--tta_k` to `parse_args` with default=1 to preserve the base command behavior. Run `python experiment.py --out_dir=run_i` for K=1, 5, 10, reporting test AUC, sensitivity, specificity, and ECE. Analyze how logit averaging smooths decision boundaries, reduces variance under minor acquisition shifts, and improves calibration without retraining. This requires only ~30 lines of targeted edits to `build_transforms` and `evaluate`.
